channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Hundreds of thousands rally in Istanbul in support of imprisoned mayor
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of protesters again congregated in Istanbul Saturday to how their support for the city’s imprisoned mayor and demand his release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Danish foreign minister scolds Trump administration for its criticism of Denmark and Greenland
item.description: The Danish foreign minister has scolded the Trump administration for its “tone” in criticizing Denmark and Greenland, saying his country is already investing more into Arctic security and remains open to more cooperation with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Philip Crowther, Kirsten Grieshaber And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK bans ninja swords in move to crack down on violent knife crime
item.description: As knife crime continues to plague the U.K., the Labour government is attempting to crack down by adding ninja swords to the list of banned knives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New US strikes against Houthi rebels kill at least 1 in Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pounded Yemen overnight into Saturday, reportedly killing at least one person as the American military acknowledged earlier bombing a major military site in the heart of Sanaa controlled by the Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nostalgia, relief and loss as some Syrians mark their first Ramadan back home in years
item.description: As they spend their first Ramadan in their country in years, many Syrians who’ve recently returned from abroad to a homeland they were forced to flee have been celebrating the end of the Assad family’s rule.
item.dc:creator: Mariam Fam, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Myanmar resistance movement announces partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts
item.description: Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government, which coordinates the popular struggle against the ruling military, has announced a unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: David Rising And Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: United Kingdom could be only G7 nation not to produce its own steel; Chinese owner blames Trump tariffs
item.description: A Chinese steel group that owns British Steel is blaming U.S. tariffs for the potential closure of two blast furnaces that could impact the steel industry in Britain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: Israel has launched an attack on Beirut, marking its first strike on Lebanon's capital since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war last November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US, Japan, Philippines stage navy drills in disputed South China Sea as a Chinese ship keeps watch
item.description: ABOARD BRP JOSE RIZAL, South China Sea (AP) — The United States, Japan and the Philippines on Friday staged joint naval drills to boost crisis readiness off a disputed South China Sea shoal as a Chinese military ship kept watch from a distance.
item.dc:creator: AARON FAVILA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs on autos, parts imports could add $4,711 to vehicle cost: Economist
item.description: Noted economist Arthur Laffer warns that President Trump's proposed 25% tariff on foreign-made vehicle imports could lead to a dramatic increase in prices and threaten the U.S. auto industry.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Putin says Trump plans for Greenland 'has nothing to do' with Russia
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says President Donald Trump's ambitions for Greenland has "nothing to do" with Russia as Vice President JD Vance lands to assess security base.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fears of civil war in South Sudan stem from political rivalry between the president and his deputy
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Tensions are rising in the oil-rich east African nation of South Sudan after Riek Machar, who serves as one of the country’s vice presidents, was arrested in the capital.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lithuanian, Polish troops aid in 'search and recovery' for 4 missing US soldiers from Fort Stewart
item.description: A Lithuanian official told Fox News Digital about ongoing search efforts to recover four missing U.S. Army soldiers in coordination with Poland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: LONDON (AP) — King Charles III was briefly hospitalized for observation on Thursday after experiencing “temporary side effects’’ related to a scheduled cancer treatment, Buckingham Palace said in a statement. His engagements for Thursday afternoon and Friday were canceled.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: His brothers’ keeper: Liran Berman calls for urgent action to save his brothers, Gali and Ziv, from Hamas
item.description: Gali and Ziv Berman were taken hostage on Oct. 7, 2023, and their brother, Liran, has been fighting for their freedom ever since. He says his brothers were "the light of every room" they walked into.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea's Kim oversees test of AI-powered suicide drones
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has overseen tests of newly developed AI-powered suicide drones and called for their increased production, North Korean state media said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation
